title: Portfolio V1
shortDescription: My first portfolio website, built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. A humble beginning to my web development journey.
description: This was my inaugural attempt at creating a personal portfolio. It featured a simple, clean design and showcased my early projects and skills. Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, it served as a great learning experience in front-end fundamentals and responsive design.

title: Ecoleta - Recycling Points
shortDescription: A platform connecting people with waste collection points, developed during Next Level Week by Rocketseat.
description: Ecoleta is an application designed to facilitate the connection between individuals or companies seeking to dispose of waste and collection points that handle specific types of residues. The project was part of Rocketseat's Next Level Week, an intensive coding event. It includes a web interface for registering collection points and a mobile app for users to find them. The backend API handles data management and geolocation features.

title: DevFinances - Personal Finance Control
shortDescription: A personal finance control application using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, focusing on local storage for data persistence.
description: DevFinances is a simple yet effective web application for managing personal finances. Users can add income and expense transactions, view their balance, and see a summary of their financial activity. All data is stored locally in the browser using Local Storage, making it a client-side only application. The project was a great exercise in DOM manipulation and basic financial logic implementation.

title: Proffy - Online Teaching Platform
shortDescription: A platform connecting students with teachers for online classes. Built with Node.js, React, and React Native.
description: Proffy is an online study platform that aims to connect students with teachers. Teachers can register their subjects, availability, and hourly rates, while students can search for available teachers based on subject, day, and time. The project includes a web interface for teachers and students, and a mobile app for students. It was developed during a Next Level Week event by Rocketseat, focusing on full-stack development with modern JavaScript technologies.

title: Letmeask - Live Q&A Platform
shortDescription: A real-time Q&A platform for content creators to interact with their audience during live streams. Built with React and Firebase.
description: Letmeask allows content creators to create Q&A rooms where their audience can ask questions. The creator can then highlight questions, mark them as answered, or delete them. It features real-time updates using Firebase Realtime Database and authentication with Google. This project was part of a Rocketseat event, focusing on React and Firebase integration for building interactive applications.

title: Be The Hero - Connecting NGOs with Donors
shortDescription: An application that connects NGOs with people willing to help financially with specific cases. Developed with Node.js, React, and React Native.
description: Be The Hero is a platform designed to help NGOs publicize cases that need financial support and connect them with potential donors. NGOs can register cases detailing the issue and the amount needed, and donors can choose which cases to support. The project includes a web interface for NGOs and a mobile app for donors. It was developed during a Rocketseat event, emphasizing full-stack JavaScript development and social impact.
